core_payload.content:
# Core Atom: Primitive Capsule Archetype

## 1. Definition
The Core Atom is the irreducible archetype of a capsule. It preserves identity, payload, semantic concentration, graph context, and integrity seal in one coherent unit.

## 2. Structural Rule
Atom semantics inherit the 5-Element law and cannot be divided further without losing core meaning. Atomic boundaries are what let humans and agents update the vault precisely instead of smearing many concepts into one unstable object.

## 3. Topological Role
Atomic capsules are execution and evidence units of the graph, while hubs aggregate and organize them.

## 4. Governance
By enforcing atom-level provenance, CapsuleOS avoids ambiguous compression, sustains reliable machine reasoning, and gives swarms bounded surfaces for safe work.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Core Atom defines the irreducible unit of meaning in CapsuleOS: one capsule with one coherent payload, one identity frame, one neuro-concentrate, one recursive context, and one integrity seal. The capsule matters because graph quality collapses when evidence, action, and aggregation blur into shapeless containers. By protecting atom semantics, the system preserves provenance granularity, precise version lineage, and machine-readable boundaries for later hubs to compose without erasing detail. Atom is therefore the minimum sovereign building block from which tasks, concepts, identities, physical objects, and larger project structures can be assembled responsibly, and it is the unit that makes bounded swarm work and safe capsule maintenance possible.
